sections_cited: Section 22 of the Civil Servants Act

chunks.text: - 1 - ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P. No.D-2023 of 2024 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with Signature(s) of Judge(s) ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Fresh case: 1.For order on Misc. No.9164/2024 (Urgency Application). 2.For order on office objection No.6 (On Maintainability of Petition). 3.For order on Misc. No.9165/2024 (Exemption Application). 4.For hearing of main case. 03.05.2024 Mr. Abdul Nabi Joyo, Advocate for Petitioner. ------------------ 1. Urgent application is granted. 3. Exemption application is granted subject to all just exceptions. 2-4. Through instant Constitution Petition, the petitioner, inter alia, seeks following reliefs:- a. To declare that impugned orders No.FIA/CCW/OPS- (S)/Office Order/2024/669-76, dated 29.01.2024, issued by the respondent No.4, is null and void liable to be cancelled, as the same is issued with malafide intention of respondent No.4 to gain ulterior motives. b. To suspend the operations of impugned orders No.FIA/CCW/OPS-(S)/Office Order/2024/669-76, dated 29.01.2024, issued by the respondent No.4, till the disposal of instant petition in the interest of justice. c. To direct the respondent No.1, to constitute a committee for impartial enquiry regarding the illegal acts of the respondents No.4, 6 & 7 and submits report before this Hon'ble Court and further Hon'ble Court may be pleased to pass the orders according to law in the light of enquiry report. d. To direct the respondents No.1&2 to produce detailed report regarding the appointment of the respondents No.4, 6 & 7, since appointment up till now, their conduct and performance report before this Hon'ble Court.

To direct the respondent No.1, to constitute a committee for impartial enquiry regarding the illegal acts of the respondents No.4, 6 & 7 and submits report before this Hon'ble Court and further Hon'ble Court may be pleased to pass the orders according to law in the light of enquiry report. d. To direct the respondents No.1&2 to produce detailed report regarding the appointment of the respondents No.4, 6 & 7, since appointment up till now, their conduct and performance report before this Hon'ble Court. Learned counsel contends that the petitioner is serving as Inspector FIA and posted in Cybercrime Reporting Centre (CCRC), Karachi, while his wife, namely, Erum Noor Mangi holds the position - 2 - of Sub-Inspector at FIA State Bank Circle (SBC), Karachi; that on 10.10.2022 respondent No.6 (Tahir Gujar-Sub Inspector, FIA) forcibly entered the petitioner’s office by breaking the locks under verbal directives of respondent No.4 (Dr. Muhammad Farooq, Director (Operations) South FIA Cybercrime Wing, Karachi) and stole the original files, seizure memos and CPU to harm the petitioner; that the respondent No.4 has gained notoriety for his involvement in accepting bribes and the petitioner filed numerous complaints against the respondents No.4 & 6 with higher authorities urging legal action against them for their corrupt practices, which resulted into impugned order, dated 29.01.2024, whereby the petitioner has been transferred from FIA CCRC, Karachi to FIA CCRC, Quetta with immediate effect and in compliance thereof he has been relieved with direction to join FIA CCRC, Quetta within 05 days. Heard, record perused. The petitioner has annexed at pages No.111 to 121 of his Petition a copy of his complaint, dated 03.04.2024, to Director General FIA, Islamabad for taking legal action against the respondent No.4, while his impugned transfer order was issued earlier on 29.01.2024. The Petitioner is admittedly a civil servant. The transfer and posting comes within the terms and conditions of service. Under Section 22 of the Civil Servants Act, 1973, the petitioner has the remedy of appeal, representation and review for the redressal of his grievances.

The transfer and posting comes within the terms and conditions of service. Under Section 22 of the Civil Servants Act, 1973, the petitioner has the remedy of appeal, representation and review for the redressal of his grievances. There is also a bar in terms of Article 212 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 to entertain such petitions. Hence, this petition is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE
